story.iWant: to configure LiteLLM Redis caching for exact response matches
story.soThat: I can reduce API calls by 10-20% for duplicate queries without quality impact

subtasks.subtask: Verify Redis is running và accessible
Check Redis connection configuration trong environment variables
Configure Redis connection parameters (host, port, password if needed)
Test Redis connectivity từ backend service
Review LiteLLM caching documentation và configuration options
Configure LiteLLM to use Redis for caching (exact match only, no semantic)
Verify caching is enabled trong LiteLLM configuration
Test caching với sample LLM calls
Implement cache key generation với namespace prefix
Ensure cache keys are unique và prevent cross-contamination
Test cache key generation với different models và queries
Set default cache TTL to 1 hour (3600 seconds)
Make TTL configurable via environment variable
Document TTL configuration và best practices
Extract cache hit/miss information từ LiteLLM responses
Add cache metrics to monitoring dashboard
Test metrics tracking với sample requests

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Redis instance được setup và configured cho LiteLLM
LiteLLM Redis caching được enable với exact match strategy (no semantic)
Cache keys được namespaced để prevent cross-contamination
Cache TTL được configured appropriately (default 1 hour)
Cache hit/miss metrics được tracked và logged
No quality degradation (exact matches = same responses)

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Epic Breakdown: LLM Optimization
doc.section: Story 1.2: LiteLLM Redis Response Caching (Exact Matches)
doc.snippet: Story 1.2 defines requirements for configuring LiteLLM Redis caching. Expected savings: $5-10/month with zero quality impact. Effort: 2 hours. Prerequisites: Story 1.1.

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Master Optimization Plan v1.1
doc.section: Phase 1: Quality-Preserving Quick Wins - Task 1.2: LiteLLM Redis Response Caching
doc.snippet: LiteLLM Redis caching requires Redis instance. Exact match strategy (no semantic) to ensure quality. Cache keys must be namespaced. Default TTL: 1 hour. Cache metrics must be tracked.

doc.title: Research Prompt: System Prompt Optimization
doc.section: 5. Prompt Caching Implementation - 5.3 LiteLLM Universal Caching
doc.snippet: LiteLLM provides unified caching across all providers. Configuration via litellm_config.yaml or environment variables. Supports Redis caching with exact match or semantic matching. Cache TTL configurable.

file.reason: Primary method to modify for LiteLLM Redis caching configuration. Currently uses LiteLLM router for API calls. Need to configure Redis caching in LiteLLM settings.
Function that sets up LiteLLM router. Need to configure Redis caching here or in LiteLLM global settings.
LiteLLM global configuration. Can set caching parameters here using litellm.cache or environment variables.
Redis connection initialization. Redis instance should already be running and accessible. Connection configuration via environment variables (REDIS_HOST, REDIS_PORT, REDIS_PASSWORD).
Function to get Redis client. Can be used to verify Redis connectivity and configure LiteLLM to use same Redis instance.
Configuration management. Can add cache TTL configuration here if needed. Environment variables are loaded here.

constraint.description: Zero quality impact required. Exact matches only (no semantic matching) to ensure 100% similarity with original responses.
Dual-mode architecture: Original mode (baseline) + Optimized mode (with caching). Feature flags allow switching and rollback.
Redis instance should already be running (used for Dramatiq message broker). Connection configuration via environment variables. No additional Redis setup required.
Exact match only (no semantic). Use "redis" cache type, not "redis-semantic". Cache keys must be namespaced to prevent conflicts with other Redis keys.
Default TTL: 1 hour (3600 seconds). Must be configurable via environment variable. Document configuration and best practices.
Must not break existing LLM calls. Caching should be transparent to callers. Test with existing agent configurations.

interface.name: make_llm_api_call()
interface.signature: async def make_llm_api_call(messages: List[Dict[str, Any]], model_name: str, response_format: Optional[Any] = None, temperature: float = 0, max_tokens: Optional[int] = None, tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None, tool_choice: str = "auto", api_key: Optional[str] = None, api_base: Optional[str] = None, stream: bool = True, top_p: Optional[float] = None, model_id: Optional[str] = None, headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, extra_headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None) -> Union[Dict[str, Any], AsyncGenerator, ModelResponse]

interface.name: setup_provider_router()
interface.signature: def setup_provider_router(api_key: Optional[str] = None, api_base: Optional[str] = None) -> Router

tests.standards: Testing standards include: Quality validation (100% similarity check for exact matches), Performance testing (cache hit rate, cost savings, latency), Integration testing (existing LLM calls, different models, agent configurations, cache expiration). Use exact match verification for quality validation. A/B testing framework for comparing original vs optimized modes.

test.idea: Unit test: Verify Redis connection và accessibility từ backend service
Integration test: Test Redis connectivity với actual Redis instance
Unit test: Verify LiteLLM cache configuration (cache type = "redis", not "redis-semantic")
Integration test: Test cache hit/miss behavior với sample LLM calls
Unit test: Verify cache key generation với namespace prefix
Integration test: Test namespace isolation (different models/queries generate different keys)
Unit test: Verify TTL configuration (default 1 hour, configurable via env var)
Integration test: Test cache expiration với TTL (wait for expiration và verify cache miss)
Unit test: Extract cache hit/miss information từ LiteLLM response metadata
Integration test: Verify cache metrics are logged và appear in monitoring dashboard
Automated test: Compare cached vs non-cached responses (exact match verification, target: 100% similarity)
A/B test: Run same queries with original and optimized modes, compare quality metrics
